title: Principles of Pharmacology
subject: Pharmacology
learning_objectives: Explain drug-receptor interactions using quantitative models of binding affinity and efficacy
Apply pharmacokinetic principles (absorption, distribution, metabolism, excretion) to predict drug concentration-time profiles
Derive and apply pharmacodynamic models relating drug concentration to clinical effect
Explain the mechanisms of action of major drug classes across therapeutic areas
Analyze the molecular basis of drug selectivity, tolerance, and resistance
Evaluate drug-drug interactions and their clinical implications
Apply the principles of rational drug design and structure-activity relationships
Interpret clinical pharmacology data including dose-response curves and therapeutic indices
